title: IntentForwarderActivity Self-Changing Data Type TOCTOU — Cross-Profile Intent Filter Bypass
root_cause: Intent.resolveTypeIfNeeded() does not cache resolved type in mType. ResolverActivity and IntentForwarderActivity call getType() multiple times per resolution. Malicious ContentProvider returns different MIME types on each call, routing intents to unintended handlers.
attack_vector: Attacker app with ContentProvider whose getType() returns different MIME types. First call returns innocuous type (text/plain) for initial resolution; subsequent calls return certificate type (application/x-x509-ca-cert) causing ResolverActivity to select CertInstallerMain.
prerequisites: Attacker app with exported ContentProvider and getType() TOCTOU
CertInstallerMain present on device (standard AOSP component)
impact: Arbitrary MIME type routing to system certificate installer (CertInstallerMain)
Cross-profile security policy bypass when work profile exists
Potential CA certificate installation with user interaction (misleading UI)
Arbitrary activity launch via type confusion in intent resolution
mitigations_bypassed: sanitizeIntent() strips component/package but NOT type — irrelevant since type comes from ContentProvider dynamically
preventIntentRedirect / creatorToken validates Intent object integrity, not external ContentProvider returns
Intent.resolveTypeIfNeeded() designed to re-query provider each time (no caching)
related_research: Flanker DEF CON 33 - Self-Changing Data Type attack pattern

verification_results.same_user_type_switch: CONFIRMED — getType() called 6+ times during single intent resolution. EvilProvider alternates text/plain and application/x-x509-ca-cert. CertInstallerMain launched automatically by ResolverActivity.
verification_results.cross_profile_forwarding: PARTIAL — IntentForwarderActivity resolves in query-activities but ResolverActivity reports 0 cross-profile resolvers. Cross-profile filter direction may need adjustment.
verification_results.cert_installer_launch: CONFIRMED — com.android.certinstaller/.CertInstallerMain started with realCallingUid=1000 (system). Payload data accessible via content:// URI with FLAG_GRANT_READ_URI_PERMISSION.

device_logs.gettype_calls: EvilProvider: getType() call #1 text/plain -> #2 application/x-x509-ca-cert -> #3 text/plain -> ... (alternating)
device_logs.certinstaller_start: ActivityTaskManager: START u0 {act=android.intent.action.VIEW dat=content://com.poc.typeswitcher/... cmp=com.android.certinstaller/.CertInstallerMain} from uid 10501 (com.poc.typeswitcher) (realCallingUid=1000)
device_logs.resolver_behavior: ResolverListAdapter: Add DisplayResolveInfo component: CertInstallerMain — auto-selected as only matching handler for cert type

suggested_fix: Cache resolved type after first resolution. Options: (1) Pin type in Intent.resolveTypeIfNeeded() by setting mType = resolved; (2) IntentForwarderActivity saves resolvedType and uses setType() before forwarding; (3) ResolverActivity calls getType() once and caches for all subsequent operations.
